title: Week of April 6, 2026
summary: This week focused on platform reliability improvements that make OpsPal more stable for your daily RevOps work. The platform now automatically cleans up outdated files and prevents startup errors, while new safeguards in Salesforce ensure Dynamic Forms compatibility and prevent record type configuration errors that could block your workflows.

entries.headline: Platform automatically cleans up old files during updates
details: Removes outdated cache files older than 7 days
Cleans up abandoned session files older than 5 minutes
Alerts you when memory usage reports become too large

entries.headline: Enhanced Salesforce compatibility and error prevention
details: Prevents field addition conflicts when Dynamic Forms is active on your pages
Automatically assigns new record types to System Administrator profile to prevent configuration errors
Converts blocking routing errors to warnings so your sessions continue running

entries.headline: Platform automatically fixes missing dependencies at startup
details: Detects and installs missing components when you start a session
Shows clear error messages and fix instructions when automatic installation fails
Runs background repairs for common dependency issues

entries.headline: Resolved false missing package errors during startup
